candidates.name: Donald J. Trump
candidates.notes: Trump scores highest on education, self-defense, and America First categories. His weakest areas are marriage (accepts same-sex marriage, appointed openly gay cabinet member) and abortion (supports exceptions, opposes federal ban). His EO 14202 on anti-Christian bias and EO 14168 on gender ideology are his strongest Christian-aligned executive actions. His first-term bump stock ban and red flag law support cost him on self-defense.

profile.education: B.S. Economics, Wharton School, University of Pennsylvania
profile.birthplace: Queens, New York City, New York

candidates.name: JD Vance
candidates.notes: Vance scores highest on self-defense (perfect 10) and education (perfect 10). His Catholic conversion through influence of Peter Thiel and study of Augustine shapes his worldview. His 'ordo amoris' argument for prioritizing nationals is distinctly Catholic. Weakest on PCH/CBG due to Catholic universalism vs narrow Anglo-Saxon framing. His 2022 reversal from federal abortion ban to states-rights cost him points.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (convert from evangelical Protestantism, formerly atheist/agnostic)
profile.education: B.A. Political Science & Philosophy, Ohio State University; J.D., Yale Law School

candidates.name: Pam Bondi
candidates.notes: Bondi scores strongest on marriage (8) due to her long record defending traditional marriage and opposing gay adoption as Florida AG. She chairs the DOJ Anti-Christian Bias Task Force under EO 14202. Weakest on PCH/CBG as she operates within standard legal frameworks rather than advocating Christian nationalism. Her decades-long prosecution career gives her credibility on law enforcement but less ideological advocacy.

profile.education: B.A. Criminal Justice, University of Florida; J.D., Stetson University College of Law

candidates.name: Pete Hegseth
candidates.notes: Hegseth scores the highest of all officials on this scorecard. His CREC (Christian Reconstructionist) denomination aligns most closely with the scorecard criteria. Perfect scores on self-defense and education. His explicit invocation of God-given gun rights, monthly Pentagon worship services, exclusion of Catholics from Good Friday service, and removal of transgender service members demonstrate the most consistently theocratic approach. His CREC church pastor has stated women should not vote. Moved family to Tennessee specifically for CREC church and school community.

profile.religion: CREC (Communion of Reformed Evangelical Churches) - Christian Reconstructionist denomination
profile.education: B.A. Politics, Princeton University; M.P.P., Harvard Kennedy School

candidates.name: Marco Rubio
candidates.notes: Rubio scores highest on education (10) and self-defense (8). His complex religious journey (Catholic-LDS-Baptist-Catholic) and Cuban immigrant heritage distinguish him. His A+ pro-life rating is strong but he compromised on federal ban for Trump platform. His Gang of Eight immigration bill (2013) is his biggest vulnerability from this scorecard's perspective, though he has since reversed. As SecState reinstated Global Gag Rule on abortion funding abroad. His Cuban heritage means he does not fit the Anglo-Saxon preference criteria.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (complex history: raised Catholic, attended LDS church ages 8-11, attended Southern Baptist church, returned to Catholicism)
profile.education: B.A. Political Science, University of Florida; J.D., University of Miami School of Law

candidates.name: Markwayne Mullin
candidates.notes: Mullin scores highest on self-defense (10), education (10), and abortion (8) - his no-exceptions abortion ban stance in 2022 is the strongest of any official on this scorecard. His Cherokee heritage is significant - he is the first Cherokee Nation member in the Cabinet. This directly contradicts the Anglo-Saxon demographic/heritage criteria in categories 5 and 7, giving him automatic false scores there. His judicial warrant requirement for ICE entry is more moderate than hardline enforcement would suggest. He voted against the Violence Against Women Act.

profile.religion: Christian (denomination not widely publicized; Southern Baptist likely based on Oklahoma background)
profile.education: Attended Missouri Valley College (did not graduate)

candidates.name: Linda McMahon
candidates.notes: McMahon scores highest on education (perfect 10) which is her direct portfolio. Her prayer guidance for schools and patriotic curriculum partnerships are significant. However, she scores among the lowest overall because her WWE business background did not require taking positions on most scorecard categories. Her 2010/2012 Connecticut Senate campaigns required moderate positioning. She is focused on dismantling the Department of Education per Trump's agenda. The 'History Rocks!' tour promotes patriotic education. Null scores reflect absence of evidence, not counter-evidence.

candidates.name: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
candidates.notes: RFK Jr. scores the lowest on this scorecard. Despite deep Catholic upbringing (rosary multiple times daily, mass twice daily in summers), his positions align with liberal Catholicism on most issues. His prior pro-abortion views drew opposition from Mike Pence's group during confirmation. He was confirmed 52-48 with only Mitch McConnell voting against among Republicans. His focus is on vaccine skepticism and health freedom, not the social conservative issues measured here. The Kennedy family legacy includes strong gun control advocacy. His environmental career was internationally focused. His Catholic faith is genuine but expressed through social justice rather than social conservatism.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (deeply devout upbringing; Kennedy family Catholicism)
profile.education: B.A. Harvard University; studied at London School of Economics; J.D., University of Virginia; LL.M. Environmental Law, Pace University

candidates.name: Scott Bessent
candidates.notes: Bessent ties with RFK Jr. for the lowest score. As the first openly gay Cabinet member in a Republican administration, he directly contradicts the marriage category criteria. His Soros Fund Management background and finance career mean he has no public record on most social conservative issues measured by this scorecard. His Huguenot Church affiliation is Protestant but the French Huguenot tradition is mainline/moderate. Confirmed 68-29 with significant bipartisan support, the widest margin of any Trump cabinet member. His policy focus is exclusively economic: tax cuts, tariff negotiations, and trade policy.

profile.religion: French Huguenot Church (Protestant; also historical Baptist family affiliation)

candidates.name: Elise Stefanik
candidates.notes: Stefanik's UN Ambassador nomination was withdrawn March 2025 due to thin House Republican majority; she remains in Congress through 2026 but will not seek reelection. She scores highest on self-defense (8) and education (8), where her grilling of Harvard and UPenn presidents on antisemitism was nationally prominent. Her evolution from moderate to MAGA Republican is notable - she voted to override Trump's border emergency veto in 2019 but later became one of his strongest allies. Her Catholic faith is not prominently referenced in her politics. She scores 0 on PCH/CBG as she does not advocate Christian nationalism.
